## [06:17 - 09:17]

And so once you bring on a business developer, you absolutely need that service document in place. And that will remove you and save a lot of the time in this process, right? And once the scope is finished, the account manager then needs to arrange a kickoff call with the client to introduce themselves and run through the scope. Just so the client knows what's happening, they feel like they're in the loop. This is a really important step. Okay. Now what I would recommend is the first two weeks of onboarding a client, the account manager should be emailing the client every single day with an update, even if nothing has happened, just a touching base. Hey, just to let you know, we're still working on this email, or we're still waiting on this and we'll keep you posted. This opens communication lines and it builds trust. It keeps clients in the loop. And as such, it will build a lot more tolerance with the client if things don't work out as well initially. Okay. So this is important as well. Another thing that I really want to stress here is that all client communications and phone calls should be going back into your CRM system. Because if you ever get in a situation of he said, she said type of thing, you really want to be able to pull up that. Information or if you ever lose a staff or something happens, you really want to be able to look at all the email correspondences. So what we do is we CC Hub spot into all of our client communication emails. It means that any emails from or to our clients goes straight into their account, their CRM file. So at any one stage, we can go in there and see every single communication anyone on our team has ever had with that client. And every phone call we call from HubSpot. So it's all recorded. Now you don't have to use HubSpot. I'm not an affiliate of HubSpot. You can just use software is like screen flow or quick time to record your calls and save that inside of a Google Drive. What I'm trying to stress here is that have some kind of system and train your team where every single communication with a client needs to be tracked back to a CRM system.

## [12:22 - 12:35]

Just to protect yourself and also to build efficiencies within your team so that people are not searching for, you know, spending hours searching for one email that was sent, you know, two months ago about something that someone said.
